text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Soft boiled eggs are ready after about five minutes of cooking","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Our timer says they've been going for almost exactly five minutes, which means I can go ahead and pull the first two of our eggs. These should be perfectly soft boiled.","entailment":"The quote states that after five minutes the eggs are pulled and are described as perfectly soft boiled, indicating that five minutes is the cooking time for soft boiled eggs.","population":"eggs","intervention":"boiling for five minutes","comparator":"","outcome":"soft boiled egg (white set, yolk runny)","timeframe":"5 minutes","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Medium boiled eggs take about seven minutes to cook","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Now, if you want a medium boiled egg, those are going to take seven minutes. So we'll wait a bit more for those to finish up. Exactly seven minutes.","entailment":"The speaker explicitly says that medium boiled eggs require seven minutes of cooking before they are done.","population":"eggs","intervention":"boiling for seven minutes","comparator":"","outcome":"medium boiled egg (outside set, inside jammy yolk)","timeframe":"7 minutes","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Hard boiled eggs require ten minutes of boiling","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Since the fully hard boiled egg is going to take ten minutes, I'll set the timer to ten.","entailment":"The speaker sets a ten‑minute timer because a fully hard boiled egg needs ten minutes of cooking.","population":"eggs","intervention":"boiling for ten minutes","comparator":"","outcome":"hard boiled egg (fully set yolk, firmer white)","timeframe":"10 minutes","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"An ice bath quickly stops the cooking process of boiled eggs","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The reason we need an ice bath is to quickly stop the cooking process, because when anything comes off the heat, it's going to continue cooking.","entailment":"The quote explains that placing boiled eggs in an ice bath halts further cooking, indicating a causal effect of the ice bath on stopping cooking.","population":"boiled eggs","intervention":"placing in ice bath","comparator":"continuing at room temperature","outcome":"stops further cooking","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"For soft boiled eggs the white is set and the yolk is runny","claimType":"empirical","quote":"For our soft boiled eggs, the white should be set, but that yolk inside should be nice and runny.","entailment":"The speaker describes the expected internal appearance of a soft boiled egg, linking the cooking method to a set white and runny yolk.","population":"soft boiled eggs","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"white set, yolk runny","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"For medium boiled eggs the outside is set and the inside is a jammy yolk","claimType":"empirical","quote":"For medium boiled eggs the outside is going to be set. But the inside is going to be that nice jammy yolk that you see all over social media.","entailment":"The quote specifies the visual characteristics of a medium boiled egg, indicating a set exterior and a jammy interior yolk.","population":"medium boiled eggs","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"outside set, inside jammy yolk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Hard boiled eggs have a fully set yolk and firmer white","claimType":"empirical","quote":"When I cut this one open, we should have a fully set yolk and the white will be firmer.","entailment":"The speaker reports that a hard boiled egg’s yolk is fully set and the white is firmer, describing the outcome of the hard‑boiling process.","population":"hard boiled eggs","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"yolk fully set, white firmer","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Adding a splash of vinegar to boiling water helps poached eggs keep a nice shape while cooking","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Add just a splash of vinegar. What the vinegar does is helps your egg keep a nice shape while it cooks.","entailment":"The quote states that vinegar has the effect of maintaining the egg’s shape during poaching, indicating a causal relationship.","population":"eggs being poached","intervention":"adding vinegar to boiling water","comparator":"","outcome":"egg keeps a nice shape","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Creating a vortex in boiling water helps keep the poached egg centered and together with vinegar produces a clean poached egg","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The next step is to create a little vortex in your pot of boiling water... which, working together with the vinegar, is going to hold everything together to get us a nice clean poached egg.","entailment":"The speaker explains that the vortex, combined with vinegar, causes the egg to stay centered and results in a clean poached egg, indicating a causal effect.","population":"eggs being poached","intervention":"creating a vortex in boiling water","comparator":"","outcome":"nice clean poached egg","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Poached eggs are ready after about two to three minutes of cooking","claimType":"empirical","quote":"After about 2 to 3 minutes, you can start thinking about pulling out your poached egg.","entailment":"The speaker indicates that a poached egg reaches doneness after roughly two to three minutes of cooking.","population":"eggs being poached","intervention":"cooking for 2–3 minutes","comparator":"","outcome":"poached egg ready","timeframe":"2 to 3 minutes","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"}]}
